title: Insecure API Endpoints
description: Learn how insecure or poorly protected API endpoints can expose sensitive data and functionality to unauthorized users.

attack_stages.title: API Exposure
attack_stages.description: The application exposes API endpoints to allow data exchange between systems and clients.

attack_stages.title: Endpoint Discovery
attack_stages.description: Attackers scan and analyze publicly accessible API endpoints.

attack_stages.title: Weak Authentication
attack_stages.description: Some API endpoints lack proper authentication mechanisms.

attack_stages.title: Authorization Bypass
attack_stages.description: Attackers access data or functions without proper permissions.

attack_stages.title: Data Exposure
attack_stages.description: Sensitive information or functionality is accessed or abused.

prevention_points.title: Require Authentication
prevention_points.description: Ensure all sensitive API endpoints require secure authentication.

prevention_points.title: Enforce Authorization Checks
prevention_points.description: Validate user permissions for every API request.

prevention_points.title: Minimize Data Exposure
prevention_points.description: Only return necessary data in API responses.

prevention_points.title: Monitor API Usage
prevention_points.description: Track and detect unusual or suspicious API activity patterns.

nodes.title: Scenario
nodes.text: A web application uses APIs to allow users and systems to interact with backend services such as retrieving or updating data.

nodes.title: What is an API?
nodes.text: An API (Application Programming Interface) allows different systems to communicate and exchange data. Web applications use APIs to fetch or modify information from backend services.

nodes.title: Insecure API Endpoints
nodes.text: Insecure API endpoints occur when APIs are exposed without proper authentication or authorization, allowing attackers to access sensitive data or functions.

nodes.title: Attacker Goal
nodes.text: The attacker wants to discover and exploit insecure API endpoints to access sensitive data or perform unauthorized actions.

nodes.title: How do attackers usually find insecure APIs?

choices.text: By scanning and analyzing exposed API endpoints

nodes.title: Try Again
nodes.text: Attackers typically analyze exposed endpoints and look for missing authentication or authorization controls.

nodes.title: Authorization Bypass
nodes.text: Due to missing or weak access controls, the attacker successfully accesses API endpoints without proper permissions.

nodes.title: Impact
nodes.text: Insecure APIs can lead to data leaks, unauthorized modifications, and exposure of sensitive system functionality.

nodes.title: How to Prevent This
nodes.text: Organizations should require authentication for all sensitive API requests, enforce strict authorization checks, limit data exposure, and continuously monitor API usage for suspicious behavior.

nodes.title: Attack Replay
nodes.text: Review how insecure API endpoints allowed unauthorized access to sensitive data and system functions.

nodes.title: Scenario Complete
nodes.text: Insecure API endpoints expose systems to unauthorized access and data leaks. Proper authentication, authorization, and monitoring are essential to secure APIs.
